{{Quote|I always thought the Flash speeders were only good for joyriding. Then the Trade Federation invaded and I found out why we keep these green genies around. I just closed my eyes, and the next thing I knew, I was ten kilometers away from Theed.|RSF lieutenant [[Gavyn Sykes/Legends|Gavyn Sykes]]|<ref name="TNEGtVaV" />}}
The '''''Seraph''-class urban landspeeder''', also known as the '''Flash speeder''', was a small [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] used by the [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Security Forces]] (RSF).

==Description==
[[File:FlashSpeeder_ep1ig.png|thumb|left|180px|Flash speeder schematics]]
The ''Seraph''-class urban landspeeder was a variant of the [[Seraph-class landspeeder|''Seraph''-class landspeeder]], which was a civilian model.

The military variant was used by the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] as a patrol craft and personnel [[Transport/Legends|transport]]. To assist in these duties, the ''Seraph'' was armed with a rotating blaster turret positioned on the rear of the vehicle. A single driver operated the ''Seraph'', and up to three passengers could be accommodated in its open-topped interior.

The vehicle's [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engines allowed it to reach a height of two meters above ground level, and gave the ''Seraph'' a maximum speed of 200 kph. Designed for urban environments, the ''Seraph'' was suited more to patrolling city streets than traversing uneven terrain.

==History==
[[File:Flashspeeder_console.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A flash speeder console display]]

Introduced in [[61 BBY/Legends|61 BBY]], the ''Seraph''-class urban landspeeder was designed to complement the larger [[V-19 landspeeder|V-19 "Gian" speeder]]. Members of the RSF had long felt that the Gian Speeder was not maneuverable enough for patrolling in cities, and their suggestions led to the creation of the vehicle. The new craft proved popular with RSF members in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], and other cities, such as [[Keren/Legends|Keren]], soon made use of the ''Seraph''.

''Seraph''-class urban landspeeders saw little combat prior to the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], their actions mainly limited to criminal pursuit and officer dispatch. Occasionally, the vehicles were deployed to hunt gun runners in the service of [[Borvo]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] or frighten away wild animals that had intruded city limits.

[[File:Flashspeeder-ug.png|thumb|200px|A flash speeder]]

During the Invasion of Naboo in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], many of the ''Seraph''-class vessels in service to the RSF were destroyed by the invading droid forces of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. As resistance cells began to form across the planet, the remaining ''Seraph''s were used to pass messages between cells and for reconnaissance missions. When [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] joined forces with the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] to ignite the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Second Battle of Theed]], ''Seraph'' landspeeders were used to transport her, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], and a strike force to Theed's secret tunnels, allowing them to invade the palace and capture [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]].

Civilians used an unarmed, unshielded model during the invasion.

Following the Battle of Naboo, heavily armored variants of the ''Seraph'' were commissioned and added to the RSF motor pool.

By [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] [[Perrive]] possessed a Flash Speeder which was finally stolen by [[Kal Skirata]] and [[Walon Vau]] after his death during the [[Mission to Coruscant (Clone Wars)|Mission to Coruscant]].<ref name="Republic Commando: Triple Zero">''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''</ref>

During the early days of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], several RSF ''Seraph'' landspeeders mysteriously disappeared only to resurface later in the hands of the fledgling [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="TNEGtVaV" />

[[File:BFN_CivSpeeder01.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The civilian version]]

==Behind the scenes==
''Seraph''-class urban landspeeders first appeared in [[1999]] with the release of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].

The speeder was also in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I: Battle for Naboo]]''.

A Flash speeder deed was a special pre-order reward from [[Wikipedia:GameStop|GameStop]] when pre-ordering the [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump to Lightspeed'']] expansion for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. Other than its unique appearance, it was like any other vehicle in-game in terms of performance. It did, however, have an ability to be quickly re-granted in-game using a special command in case your current speeder had been destroyed.
